summary: Session 1 (Audit & design-lock): de-duplicated the design-input proposal (kept the cleaner second draft as proposal of record), ran cross-provider consensus on Q1-Q7 (gpt-5-4 + gemini-pro, orchestrator excluded) with L1-L4 fed as decided context, and locked the design. Q1-Q5 and Q7 converged; Q6 (close-out gate strength) split materially and was operator-decided to hard-TTY/soft-non-TTY. Both engines flagged the same concrete defect in L3's spec mechanism (the S2 'extend the D3 check' plan is unsound: D3 is content-blind and inert on Lightweight) -- corrected to a new content-aware close-time validator; D3 left unchanged. Wrote the S1 Audit Lock block into spec.md plus verdict.md and s1-audit-record.md. Cross-provider session verification (gpt-5-4): round 1 ISSUES_FOUND (two Major doc-consistency findings), both fixed in-flight, round 2 VERIFIED.

reason.specifics: Session 2 is well-scoped Python implementation against a locked design (session type field, sN-issues.json enum + promoted fields, blessed start_session --type writer, the new content-aware close-time validator, state-derivation helper, tests). The routed ai-assignment analysis recommends Sonnet at high effort -- a cheaper, fully-capable coding model -- since no fresh architectural reasoning remains.
